senses.definition: used to emphasize that collective effort and cooperation are more effective in achieving success than individual effort

senses.definition: used to suggest that people are stronger and more likely to succeed when they are united, but weaker and more vulnerable to failure when they are divided or working against each other

senses.definition: used to emphasize the power of collective effort and unity to overcome great challenges and achieve significant results, even in the face of seemingly insurmountable obstacles

word_list.word: the voice of the people is the voice of God

senses.definition: used to emphasize the importance of listening to and honoring the desires and interests of a group of people, as they are seen as a force that represents a higher power

word_list.word: the enemy of (your|my) enemy is (your|my) friend
word_list.pronunciation: √į…™ ňą…õn…ômi  Ćv j ä…Ļ …Ēňź…Ļ ma…™ ňą…õn…ômi …™z j ä…Ļ …Ēňź…Ļ ma…™ f…Ļňą…õnd

senses.definition: used to suggest that in certain situations, it may be advantageous to form an alliance with someone who is not on friendly terms with you, as long as you both share a common enemy

examples.x: In politics, it's often true that the enemy of your enemy is your friend, and we see strange alliances formed for short-term gain.

word_list.word: the devil looks after his own
word_list.pronunciation: √į…ô dňą…õv…ôl lňą äks ňą√¶ft…ö h…™z ňąo än

senses.definition: used to highlight the concept of loyalty within certain groups or communities, where individuals support and protect each other, even if their actions are deemed negative or harmful by others

examples.x: The employees in that department act as if the devil looks after his own, covering up incompetence and wrongdoing by their coworkers rather than reporting issues through proper channels.
